query: North Korea nuclear test 2026 - any signs of imminent 7th nuclear test, activity at Punggye-ri test site, recent statements or preparations mid-2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the North Korea 7th nuclear test question as of mid-2026:

**Bottom line:** North Korea remains fully *postured* to conduct a 7th test at Punggye-ri "at a time of its choosing," but there are **no public signs of imminent test preparations**. The decision rests solely with Kim Jong Un, who has not signaled a test is near.

**Key points:**
- **Site readiness:** Tunnel No. 3 has been assessed as technically ready since ~2020–2022 by both U.S. and South Korean intelligence. A 2025 DIA report (reiterated in the CRS report published May 2026) says the site is "restored and now postured to conduct a seventh nuclear test at a time of its choosing."
- **Latest ROK intelligence (Nov 2025):** North Korea could carry out a test "in a short time" once Kim decides.
- **Mid-2026 focus shifted to fissile material, not testing:** On June 3–4, 2026, Kim inspected a new uranium enrichment facility ("Yongbyon-J") and vowed "exponential" nuclear expansion, claiming material capacity had "more than doubled." HEU stockpile is estimated to reach ~2.4–2.5 tonnes by end-2026.
- **Test may not be technically necessary:** 38 North (Feb 2026) argues prior tests may have already achieved sufficient miniaturization for the untested Hwasan-31 tactical warhead.
- **Time horizon:** An April 2026 assessment framed a 7th test as "probable within 12–18 months" — medium-term, not imminent.

**Notable gap:** I could not locate a confirmed CSIS/Beyond Parallel or 38 North satellite imagery report dated specifically June–July 2026 flagging fresh imminent-test indicators; the most recent site-specific imagery analyses I found (2024) showed only low-level maintenance.

sources.title: North Korea's Nuclear Weapons and Missile Programs (CRS IF10472, updated April 29 2026)
sources.snippet: A 2025 U.S. DIA report stated, 'North Korea has restored its nuclear test site and is now postured to conduct a seventh nuclear test at a time of its choosing.' Experts estimate fissile material for up to 90 warheads, ~50 assembled.

sources.title: N. Korea appears to be ready for nuclear test once Kim makes decision: defense intelligence
sources.snippet: North Korea appears ready to promptly carry out a seventh nuclear test using Tunnel No. 3 at Punggye-ri if Kim Jong-un decides; can be carried out in a short time (Nov 5, 2025 DIA briefing).

sources.title: N. Korea's Kim inspects new nuclear facility, vows to 'exponentially' bolster nuclear forces
sources.snippet: Kim visited a newly launched nuclear material production facility June 3, 2026, saying weapons-grade material capacity 'more than doubled' over five years and pledging exponential expansion.

sources.title: Analysis of a New DPRK Enrichment Facility (Yongbyon-J)
sources.snippet: New Yongbyon gas-centrifuge facility ~9,200 centrifuges, ~160 kg HEU/yr; total assessed capacity ~66 cascades / 21,648 centrifuges; HEU stockpile could reach ~2.4–2.5 tonnes by end-2026.

sources.title: Revisiting North Korea's Nuclear Tests (38 North)
sources.snippet: Argues North Korea may already have sufficient miniaturization from six prior tests; a 7th test for the Hwasan-31 tactical warhead 'may not be technically necessary.'

sources.title: Recent Activity Observed at Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Facility (Beyond Parallel/CSIS)
sources.snippet: Since 2020, US and ROK assess North Korea completed all required preparations for a 7th test from Tunnel No. 3; observed activity 'does not suggest imminent preparations.'

sources.title: DNI Gabbard Releases 2026 Annual Threat Assessment
sources.snippet: North Korea 'committed to expanding its nuclear arsenal'; emphasizes warhead stockpile and delivery systems; no flag of imminent nuclear test (released March 18, 2026).

sources.title: North Korea Nuclear Program — Military Assessment
sources.snippet: Seventh nuclear test 'probable within 12–18 months, likely validating a tactical warhead or MIRV-compatible design. The test site is restored and ready.'

sources.title: North Korea unveils a new plant to produce fuel for nuclear weapons
sources.snippet: Kim Jong Un inspected a new weapons-grade nuclear material plant June 3, 2026, plans to 'beef up nuclear forces at an exponential rate.'

sources.title: N Korea's Kim Jong Un calls for 'exponential' expansion of nuclear arsenal
sources.snippet: Kim cites worsening security threats and long-term confrontation with 'most ferocious enemies' to justify exponential nuclear expansion (June 4, 2026).

data_points.metric: Number of North Korean nuclear tests conducted to date
Estimated fissile material sufficient for warheads
Total assessed centrifuge count as of June 2026
Estimated cumulative HEU stockpile by end-2026 (upper)
Estimated additional annual HEU capacity from Yongbyon-J
OSINT-assessed time horizon for probable 7th test
